mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly drives equity valuations by expanding corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and weakens consumer spending power, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the predictive Rank IC for UNRATE changes exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates robust household balance sheet expansion, which amplifies discretionary spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 50bps in forward returns over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation compress real yields and increase discount rates applied to distant equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive or neutral when both series rise simultaneously, or the combined signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects resilient manufacturing output and supply chain stability, providing a positive earnings floor that supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical difference between months of industrial production growth versus contraction, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding the 600 thousand unit threshold signals sustained construction activity and household formation, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of instances where HOUST crosses above 600, or the trigger's hit rate falls below 20% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts aggregate demand and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates held at or near zero constrain the central bank's ability to stimulate growth during a liquidity trap, leading to stagnant equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally significantly when rates remain at zero, or the forward return distribution under this regime shows no negative skew compared to a neutral baseline.
